2605.02200.title: ARGUS: Policy-Adaptive Ad Governance via Evolving Reinforcement with Adversarial Umpiring
2605.02200.paper_summary: Online advertising governance faces significant challenges due to the non-stationary nature of regulatory policies, where emerging mandates (e.g., restrictions on education or aesthetic anxiety) create severe label inconsistencies and reasoning ambiguities in historical datasets. In this paper, we propose ARGUS, a policy-adaptive governance system that enables evolving reinforcement through multi-agent adversarial umpiring. ARGUS addresses the sparsity of new policy data by employing a three-stage framework: (1) Policy Seeding for initial perception; (2) Adversarial Label Rectification, which utilizes a ``Prosecutor-Defender-Umpire'' architecture to resolve conflicts between stale labels and new mandates; and (3) Latent Knowledge Discovery, which employs a tripartite dialectical discussion to unearth sophisticated, ``gray-area'' violations. By leveraging RAG-enhanced policy knowledge and Chain-of-Thought synthesis as dynamic rewards for reinforcement learning, ARGUS synchronizes its reasoning pathways with evolving regulations. Extensive experiments on both industrial and public datasets demonstrate that ARGUS significantly outperforms traditional fine-tuning baselines, achieving superior policy-adaptive learning with minimal gold data.
2605.02200.paper_summary_zh: 線上廣告治理面臨重大挑戰，因為監管政策的非靜態性質，出現的新興指令（例如，對教育或美學焦慮的限制）在歷史數據集中造成嚴重的標籤不一致和推理模糊性。在本文中，我們提出了ARGUS，一個政策自適應治理系統，通過多代理對抗裁判實現不斷演變的強化學習。ARGUS通過採用三階段框架來解決新政策數據的稀疏性：（1）政策播種以進行初步感知；（2）對抗標籤修正，利用“檢察官-辯護人-裁判”的架構來解決過時標籤與新指令之間的衝突；以及（3）潛在知識發現，通過三方辯證討論來挖掘複雜的“灰色地帶”違規行為。通過利用增強的政策知識和思維鏈合成作為強化學習的動態獎勵，ARGUS使其推理路徑與不斷演變的規範同步。在工業和公共數據集上的廣泛實驗表明，ARGUS顯著超越了傳統微調基準，以最少的黃金數據實現卓越的政策自適應學習。
2605.02200.author: Deyi Ji et.al.
2605.02200.authors: Deyi Ji, Junyu Lu, Xuanyi Liu, Liqun Liu, Hailong Zhang, Peng Shu, Huan Yu, Jie Jiang, Tianru Chen, Lanyun Zhu
